objectives: Define biodiversity and its importance.
Understand the different levels of biodiversity.
Explain the need for classifying living organisms.
Describe the principles of binomial nomenclature.
Identify the contributions of Carolus Linnaeus.
Explain the rules of International Code of Botanical/Zoological Nomenclature.
Understand the criteria used for classifying organisms.
Differentiate between various classification systems.
List the different taxonomic categories.
Explain the concept of species, genus, family, order, class, phylum, and kingdom.
Describe the characteristics of the five kingdoms: Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia.
Identify examples of organisms from each kingdom.
Classify plants into Thallophyta, Bryophyta, Pteridophyta, Gymnosperms, and Angiosperms.
Describe the key features of each plant group.
Provide examples for each plant division.
Classify animals into various phyla and classes.
Describe the distinguishing characteristics of each animal group.
Provide examples for each animal phylum/class.
Define extinct, endangered, vulnerable, and rare species.
Identify factors contributing to species endangerment.
Discuss the importance of species conservation.
topics.title_or: ବିଲୁପ୍ତ, ବିପଦଗ୍ରସ୍ତ, ଅସୁରକ୍ଷିତ ଓ ବିରଳ ଜୀବ

topics.title_en: Introduction to Cell and Cell Theory
objectives: Define a cell and its fundamental role.
Explain the cell theory and its postulates.
Differentiate between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.
Describe the structure and function of the plasma membrane.
Explain the process of osmosis and diffusion.
Identify the composition and role of the cell wall in plant cells.
Describe the structure and function of cytoplasm.
Identify and explain the roles of mitochondria, vacuoles, endoplasmic reticulum, Golgi bodies, ribosomes, lysosomes, and plastids.
Relate organelle functions to overall cell activities.
Describe the structure and function of the nucleus.
Explain the composition and role of chromosomes.
Understand the importance of DNA in genetic information.
Define tissue and its significance in multicellular organisms.
Explain the concept of tissue organization.
Describe the characteristics of meristematic tissues.
Identify different types of meristematic tissues (apical, lateral, intercalary).
Explain the role of meristematic tissues in plant growth.
Differentiate between simple and complex permanent tissues.
Describe the structure and function of parenchyma, collenchyma, and sclerenchyma.
Explain the roles of xylem and phloem in transport.
Describe the general characteristics of epithelial tissues.
Identify different types of simple and stratified epithelia.
Explain the functions of various epithelial tissues.
Describe the components and functions of connective tissues.
Explain the structure and role of blood, bone, and cartilage.
Differentiate between various types of connective tissues.
Describe the characteristics of muscle tissues.
Differentiate between striated, unstriated, and cardiac muscles.
Explain the functions of each type of muscle tissue.
Describe the structure and function of nervous tissue.
Identify the components of a neuron (cell body, dendrites, axon).
Explain how nerve impulses are transmitted.
Understand the concept of food crisis and its causes.
Identify major food grains and their importance.
Discuss strategies for increasing food production.
Explain methods for improving crop varieties.
Describe the role of plant breeding and biotechnology.
Differentiate between fertilizers and manures.
Explain the benefits and drawbacks of chemical fertilizers.
Describe the principles and advantages of organic farming.
Identify common crop pests and diseases.
Explain various methods of pest and disease control.
Understand the concept of Integrated Pest Management (IPM).
Define animal husbandry and its importance.
Discuss different aspects of livestock management.
Describe practices involved in cattle rearing for milk and draft.
Explain the management of poultry for eggs and meat.
Identify common breeds and their characteristics.
Explain the methods of pisciculture (fish farming).
Describe the importance of mollusc culture and bee-keeping.
Discuss the economic benefits of various animal rearing practices.
Define health and disease.
Explain the process of becoming diseased.
Identify the requirements for a healthy person.
Categorize different causes of diseases (e.g., pathogens, lifestyle, genetics).
Understand the concept of immediate and contributory causes.
Differentiate between communicable and non-communicable diseases.
Identify common examples of each type of disease.
Explain modes of transmission for communicable diseases.
Describe the causes, symptoms, and prevention of typhoid, diarrhea, malaria, hepatitis, rabies, AIDS, tuberculosis, and polio.
Understand the role of vaccination in disease prevention.
Explain the principles of disease treatment.
Discuss the importance of appropriate drug use.
Understand the concept of immunity and its role in health.
